patch.value: GH-607: validate reports ok:true / checked_items:0 on structurally-unparseable item .toon — silently skips what pm get hard-errors on

validate fails (ok:false) with an item-parse-integrity check naming each unparseable item file instead of skipping it; checked_items mismatch vs on-disk item files is itself a failure; repro from GH-607 (count-header corruption) becomes a regression test

GH-607. A structurally-unparseable item .toon (e.g. count header disagreeing with data lines — exactly what a botched concurrent-append merge produces, pm-9q2t) is silently skipped by pm validate: ok:true, checked_items:0, while pm get on the same item exits 1 "TOON item document is not valid TOON". The workspace-integrity command must fail on provably unreadable items; checked_items vs on-disk file-count mismatch is itself a failure. Related counts-only precedent pm-a9mc.
